actor_claims.quote_text: On May 8, 2013, the special committee held a telephonic meeting.
On May 6, 2013, our board of directors held a telephonic special meeting, attended only by the independent directors.
BofA Merrill Lynch reported that contact to our chairman and management
Gibson Dunn reviewed certain considerations related to the special committee’s fiduciary duties in connection with the strategic review process.

actor_relation_claims.quote_text: at the direction of our chairman on behalf of the special committee, BofA Merrill Lynch sent a non-disclosure agreement to Company H.
Gibson Dunn reviewed certain considerations related to the special committee’s fiduciary duties in connection with the strategic review process.
Gibson Dunn submitted to Shearman proposed revisions to the merger agreement markup received from Shearman on May 28, 2013.

representatives from Paul Hastings LLP (“Paul Hastings”), counsel to Messrs. Moshayedi, and Shearman & Sterling LLP (“Shearman”), counsel to WDC, commenced negotiating the terms of the covenant not to sue

representatives from Paul Hastings LLP (“Paul Hastings”), counsel to Messrs. Moshayedi, and Shearman & Sterling LLP (“Shearman”), counsel to WDC, commenced negotiating the terms of the covenant not to sue

bid_claims.quote_text: On May 3, 2013, WDC submitted a written indication of interest to acquire the company at a purchase price in the range of $6.60 – $7.10 per share in cash.
Also on May 10, 2013, Company D submitted a written non-binding indication of interest to acquire the company at a purchase price of $5.75 per share in cash, along with a draft exclusivity agreement.
On May 15, 2013, Company H submitted a written non-binding indication of interest to acquire the company at a purchase price in the range of $5.00 – $5.75 per share in cash.
On May 28, 2013, WDC submitted a written second-round indication of interest at a price per share of $9.15 in cash, with a mark-up of the merger agreement and drafts of ancillary agreements
On June 10, 2013, WDC submitted a revised written indication of interest to acquire the company at a price range of $6.60 to $7.10 per share in cash.
On June 14, 2013, WDC submitted a written indication of interest of $6.85 per share in cash and indicated it could be in a position to announce a transaction on June 24, 2013.

event_claims.description: Company H contacted BofA Merrill Lynch expressing interest in a potential transaction with sTec.
event_claims.quote_text: On May 1, 2013, Company H contacted representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch expressing an interest in a potential transaction with sTec.

event_claims.description: BofA Merrill Lynch sent a non-disclosure agreement to Company H at the direction of the chairman on behalf of the special committee.
event_claims.quote_text: on May 2, 2013, at the direction of our chairman on behalf of the special committee, BofA Merrill Lynch sent a non-disclosure agreement to Company H.

event_claims.description: WDC submitted a written indication of interest to acquire the company.
event_claims.quote_text: On May 3, 2013, WDC submitted a written indication of interest to acquire the company at a purchase price in the range of $6.60 – $7.10 per share in cash.

event_claims.description: sTec entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company H.
event_claims.quote_text: Also on May 8, 2013, sTec entered into a non-disclosure agreement with Company H.

event_claims.description: Company D submitted a written non-binding indication of interest to acquire the company.
event_claims.quote_text: Also on May 10, 2013, Company D submitted a written non-binding indication of interest to acquire the company at a purchase price of $5.75 per share in cash, along with a draft exclusivity agreement.

event_claims.description: Company H submitted a written non-binding indication of interest to acquire the company.
event_claims.quote_text: On May 15, 2013, Company H submitted a written non-binding indication of interest to acquire the company at a purchase price in the range of $5.00 – $5.75 per share in cash.

event_claims.description: BofA Merrill Lynch sent final round process letters and a draft merger agreement to WDC and Company D requesting responses by May 28, 2013.
event_claims.quote_text: After the meeting, at the direction of the board, BofA Merrill Lynch sent final round process letters and a draft merger agreement to WDC and Company D, requesting a response by May 28, 2013.

event_claims.description: WDC submitted a written second-round indication of interest at $9.15 per share in cash.
event_claims.quote_text: On May 28, 2013, WDC submitted a written second-round indication of interest at a price per share of $9.15 in cash, with a mark-up of the merger agreement and drafts of ancillary agreements

event_claims.description: Company D stated it needed additional time to submit a written indication of interest and mark-up of the merger agreement.
event_claims.quote_text: Also on May 28, 2013, a representative of Company D contacted representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch and indicated that Company D continued to have an interest in a potential acquisition and understood they needed to move meaningfully on value; however, the representative stated that Company D needed approximately two weeks of additional time to continue its ongoing due diligence review and to submit a written indication of interest and mark-up of the merger agreement.

event_claims.description: WDC verbally indicated that its $9.15 indication of interest was its best possible price.
event_claims.quote_text: On May 30, 2013, representatives of WDC verbally indicated that WDC’s indication of interest to acquire the company at a price of $9.15 per share was WDC’s best possible price, and reiterated the concern of WDC regarding the price.

event_claims.description: The board determined to move forward with WDC on its proposed terms.
event_claims.quote_text: Our board of directors unanimously determined to move forward with WDC on their proposed terms, including endeavoring to complete diligence and negotiation of the merger agreement and ancillary agreements to be in a position to announce a transaction on June 3, 2013.

event_claims.description: WDC submitted a revised written indication of interest in the range of $6.60 to $7.10 per share in cash.
event_claims.quote_text: On June 10, 2013, WDC submitted a revised written indication of interest to acquire the company at a price range of $6.60 to $7.10 per share in cash.

event_claims.description: WDC submitted a written indication of interest of $6.85 per share in cash.
event_claims.quote_text: On June 14, 2013, WDC submitted a written indication of interest of $6.85 per share in cash and indicated it could be in a position to announce a transaction on June 24, 2013.

event_claims.description: The special committee determined to move forward with a transaction with WDC on the terms in WDC's June 14 indication of interest.
event_claims.quote_text: the special committee unanimously determined that they believed that it was in the best interests of sTec and its shareholders to move forward with a transaction with WDC on the terms set forth in WDC’s indication of interest received on June 14, 2013, subject to negotiating final terms of the merger agreement and ancillary agreements relating to such transaction, and voted to recommend the same to the board of directors.

event_claims.description: The board authorized moving forward with WDC on the terms of WDC's June 14 proposal at $6.85 per share.
event_claims.quote_text: On June 16, 2013, as directed by our board of directors, representatives of BofA Merrill Lynch informed WDC that our board of directors had authorized moving forward with a transaction with WDC on the terms set forth in WDC’s proposal received June 14, 2013, at a price of $6.85 per share, subject to negotiating final terms of the merger agreement and ancillary agreements relating to such transaction.

participation_count_claims.quote_text: On May 15, 2013, Company H submitted a written non-binding indication of interest to acquire the company at a purchase price in the range of $5.00 – $5.75 per share in cash.
After the meeting, at the direction of the board, BofA Merrill Lynch sent final round process letters and a draft merger agreement to WDC and Company D, requesting a response by May 28, 2013.
After the meeting, at the direction of the board, BofA Merrill Lynch sent final round process letters and a draft merger agreement to WDC and Company D, requesting a response by May 28, 2013.
